sections.instruction: Questions 1-5: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 6-10: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/ OR A NUMBER for each answer.

questions.content: What is the caller’s surname?
The caller says the correct color of his car is
What time does the caller say he went to collect his car?
Where does the caller usually store the front panel of his radio?
What does the advisor say she will send through the mail to the caller?

sections.instruction: Questions 11-15: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 16-20: Label the map below. Write the correct letter A-H next to questions 16-20.

questions.content: How many patients does the hospital consult every year?
When can patients meet the female doctor?
options: C only on Mondays and Fridays

questions.content: Who is the expert on treating hearing loss?
Where are patients recommended to buy their medicine?
options: A the supermarket in the town
B pharmacies nearby the city centre

questions.content: What will the patients be asked about whether they are willing to do?
options: A letting one student attend the consultation
B asking postgraduate students to do treatment
C meeting students in group discussion

sections.instruction: Choose the correct letter A, B or C.

questions.content: The man wants information on course for
options: A people going back to college

questions.content: The ‘Study for success’ seminar lasts for
In the seminar the work on writing aims to improve
Reading sessions help students to read

options: B advice on coping with stress

questions.content: The man chooses the ‘Study for success’ seminar because
options: B he wants to start at the beginning
C he seeks to revise his skills

sections.instruction: Complete the lecture notes below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context:
Public speaking
• Public speaking means speaking to (31) {{BLANK}} people
• Lack of confidence usually due to (32) {{BLANK}}
A. Planning
• First part of public speaking is (33) {{BLANK}}
• This includes (34) {{BLANK}} and length of talk
• Make speech notes on small cards to talk from (35) {{BLANK}} with the audience (very important)
B. Voice
• Speak slowly – this give time for pronunciation and is easier for audience
• Bigger audience requires (36) {{BLANK}} speech
• Project your voice – rehearse and (37) {{BLANK}}
• Check intonation: varied tone and rhythm gives (38) {{BLANK}}
C. Body language
• Lastly think about your (39) {{BLANK}} and gestures
• Show confidence by: head up, chin out, shoulders back
• Avoid scratching and fiddling because this (40) {{BLANK}} and irritates your audience

B. Voice

• Bigger audience requires (36) {{BLANK}} speech
• Project your voice – rehearse and (37) {{BLANK}}
• Check intonation: varied tone and rhythm gives (38) {{BLANK}}
C. Body language

• Show confidence by: head up, chin out, shoulders back
• Avoid scratching and fiddling because this (40) {{BLANK}} and irritates your audience
